Title: Haustoria – arsenals during the interaction between wheat and Puccinia striiformis f. sp. tritici

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing compromised Pst growth and development in wheat
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential HIGS target for reducing pathogen colonization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Predicted to encode an aspartic protease that may contribute predominantly to proteolytic activities
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_11012 is haustoria-upregulated. 2. It is predicted to encode an aspartic protease involved in proteolytic activity. 3. HIGS knockdown reduced fungal growth in wheat.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nutrient acquisition and pathogen development via proteolysis
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection by Pst race CYR31

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively affects fungal development and pathogenicity; silencing reduced hyphal growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for HIGS-based disease management
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Annotated as a ubiquitous heat shock protein containing a DnaJ domain, likely involved in protein folding, unfolding, transport and degradation in haustoria
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_22758 is highly induced in haustoria. 2. It encodes a DnaJ-domain heat shock protein linked to protein quality control. 3. HIGS knockdown reduced Pst pathogenicity and fungal growth.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection by Pst race CYR31

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing compromised Pst growth and development in wheat
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential HIGS target for stripe rust control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific molecular annotation not provided in main text
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_6346 was among haustoria-upregulated metabolism-related genes. 2. Its silencing reduced fungal growth in wheat. 3. Exact metabolic function was not detailed in the main text.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection by Pst race CYR31

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing compromised Pst growth and development in wheat
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential HIGS target for stripe rust control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific molecular annotation not provided in main text
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_15148 was among haustoria-upregulated metabolism-related genes. 2. HIGS knockdown reduced fungal growth in wheat. 3. Exact biochemical role was not given in the main text.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection by Pst race CYR31

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection of susceptible wheat by Pst race CYR31
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced hyphal growth and pathogen development in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly up-regulated in haustoria; significantly up-regulated at 18 hpi with a peak at 48 hpi
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_12394 was highly expressed in haustoria during early infection. 2. It was annotated as a glucose-6-phosphate 1-epimerase involved in glycolysis/carbohydrate metabolism. 3. HIGS silencing reduced hyphal length and pathogen growth in wheat, supporting a positive role in nutrient-supported development.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; RT-qPCR expression profiling; BSMV-mediated host-induced gene silencing; histological observation of hyphal growth
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for host-induced gene silencing or RNAi-based disease control to restrict pathogen nutrient metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Text discusses conversion of glucose-6-phosphate to fructose-6-phosphate in glycolysis; exact enzyme nomenclature in paper may reflect isomerase-like function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen growth and expansion in wheat by supporting glycolytic metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports energy generation and pathogen development
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Interconverts glucose-6-phosphate and fructose-6-phosphate in central carbohydrate metabolism

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection of susceptible wheat by Pst race CYR31
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced pathogen growth and development in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_16188 was strongly induced in haustoria and during infection. 2. It was annotated as involved in thiamine biosynthesis and related to metallothioneins. 3. HIGS silencing reduced fungal growth, suggesting thiamine-associated metabolism is important for haustorial development.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; RT-qPCR expression profiling; BSMV-mediated host-induced gene silencing; histological observation
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate pathogen metabolic target for HIGS-based stripe rust control
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussion suggests role may involve thiamine uptake/metabolism rather than a complete autonomous thiamine pathway in the pathogen
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes pathogen development, likely through thiamine-associated metabolism in haustoria
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Essential cofactor for multiple metabolic enzymes; supports growth and possibly oxidative stress handling

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Haustorial stage during infection of susceptible wheat by Pst race CYR31
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing reduced pathogen growth and development in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Up-regulated from urediospore germination with peak at late infection stage
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, RT-qPCR; no public accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Pst_19493 was up-regulated in haustoria and during infection. 2. Annotation linked it to biosynthesis of carbohydrates, amino acids and thiamine. 3. HIGS knockdown reduced pathogen growth in wheat.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq differential expression; RT-qPCR; BSMV-HIGS; histological observation
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Primary metabolism including carbohydrate/amino acid/thiamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for RNAi-based control of stripe rust
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Specific biochemical activity was not resolved in the text
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports pathogen growth and colonization in wheat
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports fungal primary metabolism and development
